steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content:
At the intersection of transversal $t$ with line $m$, angle $x^{\circ}$ is located on the left side of $t$.

At the intersection of transversal $t$ with line $n$, angle $33^{\circ}$ is located on the right side of $t$.

Both angles lie in the interior region between the two parallel lines.

This makes them co-interior angles (also called consecutive interior angles or same-side interior angles).

steps.title: Apply the parallel lines property
steps.content:
When a transversal intersects two parallel lines, co-interior angles are supplementary — they sum to $180^{\circ}$.
$$x^{\circ} + 33^{\circ} = 180^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Subtract $33^{\circ}$ from both sides:
$$x = 180^{\circ} - 33^{\circ} = 147^{\circ}$$
That matches Choice D — $147$.
